shortDescription.text: Tool handlers must not pass handler parameters to child_process APIs

fullDescription.text: exec/spawn with user input is the canonical command-injection vector. An MCP client can craft arguments that break out of the shell context and run arbitrary commands on the server host.

help.text: exec/spawn with user input is the canonical command-injection vector. An MCP client can craft arguments that break out of the shell context and run arbitrary commands on the server host.

shortDescription.text: HTTP transport enabled without any built-in auth middleware imported

fullDescription.text: If http is true in xmcp.config and nothing under src/ imports an auth middleware, the server exposes tools anonymously. This is rare enough to flag high severity — use --disable-rule when auth lives in a reverse proxy.

help.text: If http is true in xmcp.config and nothing under src/ imports an auth middleware, the server exposes tools anonymously. This is rare enough to flag high severity — use --disable-rule when auth lives in a reverse proxy.
help.markdown:
If http is true in xmcp.config and nothing under src/ imports an auth middleware, the server exposes tools anonymously. This is rare enough to flag high severity — use --disable-rule when auth lives in a reverse proxy.

**Bad**
```ts
http: true,  // + no auth middleware imported anywhere
```

**Good**
```ts
import { apiKeyAuthMiddleware } from "xmcp";
// or jwtAuthMiddleware
```

shortDescription.text: package.json must have a name and version to publish an MCP server

fullDescription.text: MCP server manifests require `name` and `version` for registry publication and for clients to identify the server.

help.text: MCP server manifests require `name` and `version` for registry publication and for clients to identify the server.
help.markdown:
MCP server manifests require `name` and `version` for registry publication and for clients to identify the server.

**Bad**
```ts
{ "scripts": { "dev": "xmcp dev" } }
```

**Good**
```ts
{ "name": "my-server", "version": "0.1.0" }
```

shortDescription.text: zod string fields should cap length with .max()

fullDescription.text: An unbounded string field lets a caller paste megabytes of text into the handler. That wastes the model's context budget and gives an attacker cheap amplification for other abuses.

help.text: An unbounded string field lets a caller paste megabytes of text into the handler. That wastes the model's context budget and gives an attacker cheap amplification for other abuses.
help.markdown:
An unbounded string field lets a caller paste megabytes of text into the handler. That wastes the model's context budget and gives an attacker cheap amplification for other abuses.

**Bad**
```ts
name: z.string().describe("...")
```

**Good**
```ts
name: z.string().max(200).describe("...")
```

shortDescription.text: Tool, prompt, or resource handlers must not use synchronous fs APIs

fullDescription.text: Synchronous I/O blocks Node's event loop. When multiple tool calls run concurrently on the same server, a single readFileSync stalls every in-flight request.

help.text: Synchronous I/O blocks Node's event loop. When multiple tool calls run concurrently on the same server, a single readFileSync stalls every in-flight request.

message.text: exec() argument derives from handler input — command injection

description.text: Use execFile with an argv array

message.text: HTTP transport is enabled but no xmcp auth middleware is imported in src/

description.text: Import apiKeyAuthMiddleware or jwtAuthMiddleware from "xmcp", or disable this rule if auth is terminated upstream

message.text: readFileSync() blocks the event loop in a handler

description.text: Add the required fields to package.json

message.text: Schema field "name" is an unbounded z.string()
